markets.question: WTT - Men's Singles: New Zealand vs Morocco
markets.description:
This market refers to the table tennis match between New Zealand and Morocco in a WTT event, scheduled for April 30 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Zealand' if New Zealand wins against Morocco.

This market will resolve to 'Morocco' if Morocco wins against New Zealand.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: Will South Korea ETF (EWY) hit (HIGH) $158 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for South Korea ETF (EWY) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the South Korea ETF (EWY) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.EWY%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Rounds?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined rounds played by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined rounds" is the sum of rounds won by both teams during the match, including any overtime rounds if applicable. For example, a match ending 13–11 would have a total of 24 rounds (even), while a match ending 13–10 would have a total of 23 rounds (odd).

If Map 1 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Privateer Gaming vs Hermine Esports Club (BO3) - Exort Series Contenders Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 2 match between Privateer Gaming and Hermine Esports Club in the Exort Series Contenders Stage, initially scheduled for April 30 at 2:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Privateer Gaming" if Privateer Gaming win the match against Hermine Esports Club.

This market will resolve to "Hermine Esports Club" if Hermine Esports Club win the match against Privateer Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the Exort Series #26 Contenders Stage Swiss bracket, both Privateer Gaming and Hermine Esports Club enter this Round 2 BO3 matchup at 0-1 after losses yesterday—Privateer fell 0-2 to FORZE Reload on Ancient, Nuke, and Overpass, while Hermine suffered a forfeit defeat to Oldboys. A loss here risks early elimination, with top 8 advancing to the Main Stage from the 16-team field. Hermine boasts stronger recent form, going 3-0 in Exort #25 Contenders Stage with wins over P2N, KUUSAMO, and 3DMAX Academy, backed by stable roster xReal, RiiL3, kdaN, fiction, and Toshas. Privateer, a lower-tier squad lacking HLTV ranking, shows poor results from ESEA Entry Division losses late last year, highlighting stylistic and experience gaps in this online European C-Tier event.

markets.question: Will the Bahujan Samaj Party (BSP) win the most seats in the 2026 Puducherry Legislative Assembly election?
markets.description:
Legislative Assembly elections are to be scheduled to be held in Puducherry, India, in April–June 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly in the 2026 election.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. If that also results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by the Indian government, specifically the Election Commission of India (ECI) (https://eci.gov.in). If multiple official reports differ, this market will resolve based on the one that includes the greatest number of Assembly Constituencies (ACs).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent All India N.R. Congress (AINRC), led by Chief Minister N. Rangasamy and allied with BJP in the NDA, commands overwhelming trader consensus at 96.3% implied probability of emerging as the largest party in the Puducherry Legislative Assembly following the April 9 voting, driven by unanimous exit polls released April 29 projecting NDA wins of 15-25 seats out of 30. Agencies like Axis My India (16-20 seats), People Pulse (16-19), and others highlight AINRC's edge from contesting 16 seats, incumbency advantages, and opposition fragmentation among INC-DMK (4-13 seats projected) and debutant TVK (0-4 seats), amid record 89.8% voter turnout. Actual results on May 4 could shift odds via counting discrepancies, TVK surges, or alliance disputes, though poll consensus suggests slim upset risk.

markets.question: Map 2: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 2 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 30°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

Competitive positioning in the space economy, fueled by orbital data centers and AI satellite deployments, supports elevated caps, though regulatory reviews and potential Starship delays could pressure lower strikes like 1.8T–2.0T (13%). markets.question: Will Iran strike Spain by April 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on the listed country's soil or any official embassy or consulate of the listed country between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Iranian military forces that impact a listed country's ground territory or any official embassy or consulate of that country (e.g., if a weapons depot on a listed country soil is hit by an Iranian missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on a listed country's territory or cause damage.

Strikes within the West Bank or the Gaza Strip, will be counted as Israel.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US or Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by the end of the third calendar date after this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

eventMetadata.context_description: US Lecce leads trader consensus at 40.5% implied probability for the Serie A relegation scrap at Pisa's Arena Garibaldi, buoyed by head-to-head dominance—capped by their 1-0 home win over Pisa in December—and a three-point edge in 17th (29 points) over rock-bottom Pisa (18 points). Pisa's six-match losing run, worst defensive record (61 conceded), and key absences (Tramoni muscular, Marin, Denoon injuries) undermine their must-win home push, despite just two Arena wins all season and frequent blanks. Lecce's recent 0-0 draw at Verona via VAR highlights grit, though low scorers (22 goals) and injuries (Berisha, Gaspar, Fofana out) temper expectations for a cagey, low-scoring draw at 31.5%.

markets.question: Will the Boston Celtics win the NBA Eastern Conference Finals?

events.title: NBA Eastern Conference Champion
events.description: This is a market on which team will win the Eastern Conference Finals in the 2025–26 NBA season.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Celtics hold the top trader consensus at 47% implied probability for Eastern Conference champion amid first-round playoffs where they lead Philadelphia 3-2 after dropping Game 5, buoyed by dominant regular-season 56-26 mark and Jayson Tatum's strong return. Cleveland Cavaliers sit at 23% following their 125-120 Game 5 win over Toronto for a 3-2 edge, aided by Raptors injuries to Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram. New York Knicks command 18% after Jalen Brunson's 39-point explosion in a 126-97 Game 5 rout of Atlanta, also up 3-2. Top seed Detroit Pistons lag at 9% despite Cade Cunningham's 45-point Game 5 forcing Orlando's Game 6 with Magic's Franz Wagner sidelined, highlighting upset risks in tight series.

markets.question: Leeds United FC vs. Burnley FC: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Leeds United FC and Burnley FC, scheduled for May 1 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Leeds United FC and Burnley FC combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Leeds United host relegation-threatened Burnley at Elland Road on May 1 in a Premier League clash with home-side momentum from their shock 2-1 win at Manchester United on April 13, boosting mid-table security at 15th with 36 points from 32 games. Burnley languish in 19th after a 0-2 home defeat to Brighton on April 11, winless in six amid 4-8-20 record, heightening desperation ahead of tough fixtures at Nottingham Forest and Manchester City. Leeds face defensive headaches with Joe Rodon and Anton Stach sidelined until early May via ankle injuries, plus Daniel James out; Burnley miss Hannibal Mejbri (hamstring), Axel Tuanzebe (Achilles), and captain Josh Cullen (ACL) long-term. Head-to-head favors Burnley after October's 2-0 win, but Elland Road form and rest advantage tilt trader consensus toward the Whites.

eventMetadata.context_description: With Burnley and Wolverhampton Wanderers mathematically relegated after dismal campaigns, the Premier League relegation battle centers on Tottenham Hotspur (34 points from 34 games) and West Ham United (36 points), separated by just two points in 17th and 18th on the table. Spurs secured their first league win of 2026—a narrow 1-0 victory over Wolves last weekend—while West Ham grabbed a dramatic stoppage-time winner against Everton, boosting trader consensus on their survival chances amid poor goal differences (-10 and -16). Nottingham Forest (39 points) and Leeds United (40) have pulled five and six points clear via recent form surges, including Forest's 5-0 thrashing of Sunderland. All face grueling run-ins against top-half sides like Arsenal, Chelsea, and Aston Villa, with key head-to-heads (Spurs vs Leeds, West Ham vs Leeds) looming that could decide the final drop spot.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price an 83.5% implied probability against a Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026, reflecting consensus for steady or modestly easing monetary policy amid resilient economic data. The April 29 FOMC statement held the federal funds target at 3.50%-3.75%—its third consecutive pause—noting solid GDP expansion, strong nonfarm payrolls, and a 4.3% unemployment rate, while inflation remains elevated per March 2026 CPI at 3.3% year-over-year. The March dot plot median projects one 25-basis-point cut by year-end, aligning with CME FedWatch futures implying a steady ~3.6% path. Key catalysts include upcoming April CPI (May 12 release) and June FOMC, where hotter data could test easing expectations but hikes remain off the table absent wage or inflation surges.

Trader sentiment hinges on Woo's edge, Seattle's pitching depth, and Royals' road struggles early in the season.

markets.question: Will Discord’s market cap be $30B or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on Discord's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by June 30, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on Discord’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an 78% implied probability against a Discord IPO by June 30, 2026, reflecting stalled momentum since the company's January confidential SEC filing with Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan—no public S-1, roadshow, or pricing announcements have emerged in the ensuing four months, narrowing the execution window amid volatile tech IPO conditions. Secondary market valuations hover around $9 billion, as indicated by recent Forge ($31/share) and Hiive ($41/share) pricing, supporting 19% odds for a sub-$15 billion debut and underscoring revenue pressures at an estimated $725 million ARR on 200 million monthly active users, with low ARPU constraining multiples versus 2021's $15 billion peak. Higher brackets below 3% await monetization catalysts like enterprise pivots.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Israel as the clear televote frontrunner at 32.5% implied probability for Eurovision 2026, fueled by Noam Bettan's emotive "Michelle"—unveiled in late February—which has surged in fan polls and betting markets amid strong diaspora mobilization patterns seen in recent contests. Greece's Akylas follows closely at 20.5% with the high-energy techno-pop "Ferto," a Sing For Greece winner boasting viral pre-party buzz and hyper-modern Greek flair reminiscent of past televote smashers. Finland's duo Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen trail at 19.5%, propelled by their record-breaking UMK public vote for violin-driven "Liekinheitin," though recent pre-parties highlight jury-televote splits. With first rehearsals underway and semis on May 12-14, staging reveals and diaspora turnout remain key swing factors in this wide-open field.

markets.question: Will Liverpool qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Conference League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Conference League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 English Premier League season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Conference League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or the EPL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Netanyahu out by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remains in office amid intensifying domestic pressure from his corruption trial, which resumed on April 28 after a two-month delay due to the Iran war and security concerns, prompting protests outside the Tel Aviv court and session shortenings for Gaza aid consultations. Recent polls indicate shrinking Knesset support for his coalition, with opposition leaders like Yair Lapid calling for no-confidence votes amid backlash over prolonged conflicts in Gaza, Lebanon, and post-ceasefire Iran tensions, including a Hezbollah tunnel destruction. Declining public approval and international scrutiny, including U.S. President Trump's public rebukes on Lebanon operations, heighten risks of snap elections or government collapse, though Netanyahu's testimony continuation and military escalations sustain his position for now. Traders weigh these catalysts against historical coalition resilience in crisis.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 20°C on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance XRP/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from AtlasIntel (April 22-27), Nexus, and Quaest consistently position incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva first in first-round voting intentions at 37-47%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro consolidating as the clear second at 32-40%, narrowing the gap to under 7 points. This trader consensus, reflected in Flávio's 66% implied probability for second place, stems from his rise leveraging the Bolsonaro family brand amid former President Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility, while Lula's 21.5% reflects low odds of him slipping amid fragmented opposition. Lower shares for Renan Santos (5%) and Fernando Haddad (4.4%) align with their distant third-place showings in multi-candidate scenarios. Traders await July party conventions and further polling ahead of the October 4 first round.

markets.question: Will Jonas Wind be the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which player will finish as the top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season.

If the listed player finishes as the sole top goal scorer in the 2025–26 Bundesliga season, the market will resolve to "Yes". Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

Goals scored in Bundesliga matches only will count. Goals in other competitions (e.g., DFB-Pokal, German Super Cup, Champions League, Europa League, international matches) will not count for this market.

If multiple players tie for the top goal scorer, the market will resolve to the player whose last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025–26 Bundesliga season is canceled or not completed by August 1, 2026, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official statistics from the Bundesliga. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane's insurmountable 33-goal haul in the Bundesliga 2025/26 season, far ahead of Deniz Undav's 18, underpins his 99.4% trader consensus as top goalscorer, with Bayern Munich having clinched the title four matches early after Kane's strike in their 4-2 win over Stuttgart on April 20. His record-breaking campaign, including recent tallies against Mainz and Stuttgart, has widened the gap amid chasers' inconsistent form and tougher remaining fixtures. Only an unprecedented injury sidelining Kane for Bayern's final games—coupled with a rival netting multiple hat-tricks—could realistically challenge, though such scenarios remain highly improbable given his fitness recovery from early-April ankle issues and Bayern's dominance.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: Spirit Academy vs Wave Esports (BO3) - Exort Series Main Stage
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Round 1 match between Spirit Academy and Wave Esports in the Exort Series Main Stage, initially scheduled for April 16 at 11:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit Academy" if Spirit Academy win the match against Wave Esports.

This market will resolve to "Wave Esports" if Wave Esports win the match against Spirit Academy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spirit Academy enters the Exort Series #25 Main Stage BO3 matchup against Wave Esports with a refreshed roster since February, anchored by veteran rifler Kiryasoo (0.98 HLTV rating over 45 maps) and coached by S0tF1k (59% career win rate), leveraging Team Spirit's academy infrastructure for structured CS2 play. Wave Esports, ranked #149 globally, fields a March-overhauled lineup featuring newcomers Belof (1.05 rating), Katalic, and sheezy alongside miksozzz, amid a 37.5% win rate in recent tier-3 events like Urban Riga Open and Esplay Backforce Masters. In Swiss Round 1, factors include adaptation to CS2 meta on maps like Dust II, Mirage, and Ancient, pistol round execution, and roster synergy, with no recent head-to-head history shaping trader consensus.

markets.question: Will Natural Gas (NG) hit (LOW) $1.60 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of Natural Gas futures has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For Natural Gas futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For Natural Gas (NG) futures, the last trading day is defined as the third last business day of the month prior to the contract month, consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month Natural Gas futures "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=ngd, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published for the Active Month Natural Gas (NG) futures contract by CME Group may be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

eventMetadata.context_description: Henry Hub natural gas spot prices settled around $2.72/MMBtu on April 29, 2026, reflecting trader consensus on ample inventories and subdued spring demand amid mild weather. The latest EIA storage report for the week ending April 24 showed a 79 Bcf build to 2,142 Bcf, below prior weeks' pace but signaling continued replenishment above five-year averages, pressuring prices down 7% over the past month. Steady U.S. production near 110 Bcf/d and resilient LNG exports around 14 Bcf/d provide supply tailwinds, though global warmth has curbed international buying. Key catalysts ahead include Thursday's EIA report, early summer cooling demand forecasts, and potential output adjustments as margins tighten.

markets.question: Leeds United FC vs. Burnley FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game between Leeds United FC and Burnley FC, scheduled for May 1 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Leeds United FC and Burnley FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Leeds United FC vs. Burnley FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for May 1 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Leeds United host relegation-threatened Burnley at Elland Road on May 1 in a Premier League clash with home-side momentum from their shock 2-1 win at Manchester United on April 13, boosting mid-table security at 15th with 36 points from 32 games. Burnley languish in 19th after a 0-2 home defeat to Brighton on April 11, winless in six amid 4-8-20 record, heightening desperation ahead of tough fixtures at Nottingham Forest and Manchester City. Leeds face defensive headaches with Joe Rodon and Anton Stach sidelined until early May via ankle injuries, plus Daniel James out; Burnley miss Hannibal Mejbri (hamstring), Axel Tuanzebe (Achilles), and captain Josh Cullen (ACL) long-term. Head-to-head favors Burnley after October's 2-0 win, but Elland Road form and rest advantage tilt trader consensus toward the Whites.

markets.question: Will xAI have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Anthropic a 65.8% implied probability of fielding the top large language model by June 30, driven by its April 16 release of Claude Opus 4.7—the firm's most capable public model to date, achieving 87.6% on SWE-bench coding benchmarks—and the preview of Claude Mythos, scoring 93.9% while excelling in vulnerability detection. These advances solidify Anthropic's competitive edge over prior leaders like Google's Gemini 3 Pro, now in a 25.5% second place amid its preview deprecation earlier this month. OpenAI trails at 6.5% as GPT iterations lag on recent LMSYS-style evaluations, with xAI's Grok-4 at 2.4% pending major leaps. Traders eye June releases and arena leaderboard shifts as key catalysts in this fluid AI race.

eventMetadata.context_description: City Councilmember Nithya Raman leads trader consensus at 54.5% implied probability for the Los Angeles mayoral primary on June 2, driven by her recent fundraising surge topping incumbent Karen Bass and a controversial late-March poll showing her ahead at 33%, positioning her as a progressive challenger dubbed "the next Mamdani" amid voter frustration with homelessness and crime. Reality TV star Spencer Pratt follows at 24%, boosted by viral campaign ads highlighting politician mansions and his personal story from the Palisades Fire, outpacing Bass's 20% amid her unfavorable ratings and 40% undecided voters in the April UCLA Luskin poll. In this nonpartisan top-two race, momentum from fundraising and undecideds keeps the field volatile ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Netanyahu out by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Benjamin Netanyahu ceases to be Prime Minister of Israel for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Benjamin Netanyahu and the government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Netanyahu out by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Benjamin Netanyahu announces that he will resign as Prime Minister of Israel, or otherwise steps down from/is removed from this position by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Note that an announcement of Benjamin Netanyahu's resignation or removal before the resolution date will suffice to resolve this market to "Yes" regardless of if/when he actually steps down.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of the State of Israel, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu remains in office amid intensifying domestic pressure from his corruption trial, which resumed on April 28 after a two-month delay due to the Iran war and security concerns, prompting protests outside the Tel Aviv court and session shortenings for Gaza aid consultations. Recent polls indicate shrinking Knesset support for his coalition, with opposition leaders like Yair Lapid calling for no-confidence votes amid backlash over prolonged conflicts in Gaza, Lebanon, and post-ceasefire Iran tensions, including a Hezbollah tunnel destruction. Declining public approval and international scrutiny, including U.S. President Trump's public rebukes on Lebanon operations, heighten risks of snap elections or government collapse, though Netanyahu's testimony continuation and military escalations sustain his position for now. Traders weigh these catalysts against historical coalition resilience in crisis.

markets.question: Will Drake feature Pimmie on ICEMAN?

To qualify as "featured", the listed artist must be credited on at least one song on the album according to at least one major streaming platform: namely Spotify, Apple Music, Amazon Music, or YouTube Music.

If the album fails to release by December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source of this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Drake's enigmatic rollout for his ninth studio album ICEMAN, confirmed for May 15 release via a massive Toronto ice installation and Instagram post, has traders betting heavily on frequent collaborators teased in recent previews like "Which One" with Central Cee and "Dog House" alongside Yeat, plus promotional zine imagery featuring Sexyy Red. These signals, combined with Drake's history of stacking OVO affiliates such as 21 Savage and Travis Scott on melodic rap cuts, drive trader consensus toward rising rap and R&B talents amid rumors of a unique beat-switch format and underground guests. No official tracklist exists, leaving room for upsets, but pre-release leaks or final promos could shift dynamics before streaming platforms like Spotify lock in credits for resolution.

markets.question: Will Venezia achieve promotion from Serie B to Serie A for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from Serie B to Serie A for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the 2025-2026 league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in Serie A for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the Serie B, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by Serie A and Serie B.

If the 2025-2026 Serie B season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from Serie A and Serie B. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Venezia has secured automatic promotion to Serie A as Serie B champions with 78 points after 36 matchdays, capping an eight-match winning streak that solidified their dominance. Monza and Frosinone sit tied on 75 points battling for the second direct spot with two games left, Frosinone holding a head-to-head advantage amid both teams' strong recent form. Palermo leads playoff contenders at 69 points, followed closely by Catanzaro (59), Modena (52), Juve Stabia (50), and Avellino (46), setting up a competitive postseason tournament post regular-season finale on May 8 to crown the third promoted side. Playoff dates were confirmed April 16, heightening focus on final standings and momentum.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices unidentified moderate Candidate M as the narrow frontrunner at 49.5% to win Colombia's May 31 presidential first-round election, reflecting fragmentation on the right and uncertainty in polls favoring Historic Pact's Iván Cepeda Castro at 38.5%. Cepeda's surge to 44% in the April 26 Invamer poll stems from President Petro's 49% approval rebound, Clara López's April 6 endorsement after dropping out, and his April 22 debate challenge to rivals. Centro Democrático's Paloma Valencia (33.7%) gains from her dominant March primaries win, while independent Abelardo de la Espriella (28.5%) slips amid right-wing splits. Recent highway bombings killing 20 highlight security as a swing issue ahead of potential June 21 runoff.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Tokyo be 7°C or below on May 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 1 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japan Meteorological Agency's latest short-term forecast pins Tokyo's lowest temperature on May 1 at 12°C, driving the market's 97.5% implied probability on this outcome amid a cool air mass from recent April 29-30 showers and high morning precipitation odds (90-100%) transitioning to clouds. Ensemble model consensus supports this, with light winds and partial cloud cover expected to suppress nocturnal radiative cooling below 12°C, while urban heat island effects at Haneda Airport station—the resolution site—align with historical early-May norms around 13-14°C slightly depressed by current synoptic patterns. Realistic challenges include unexpected overnight clearing for a 11°C drop or warmer advection pushing to 13°C, with final clarity from pre-dawn observations.

markets.question: Will President Trump sign 3 pieces of legislation into law in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of pieces of legislation that are signed into law between April 1, 2026, 12:00 AM and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Qualifying legislation may include joint resolutions and must pass both the House and the Senate, and must be signed by the President. 

Legislation that becomes law without a signature while Congress remains in session, or becomes law through veto override, as well as Presidential pocket vetoes that expire, will not qualify.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from the White House (https://www.whitehouse.gov/), Congress.gov’s legislation tracker (https://www.congress.gov/bill/119th-congress/house-bill/22), the Library of Congress (congress.gov), and other official information from the government of the United States; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket strongly favors President Trump signing exactly 6 pieces of legislation into law in April 2026 at 80% implied probability, driven by six confirmed bill signings amid a light congressional calendar typical for late-spring midterms. Key recent actions include H.R. 7147 signed on April 30, H.J.Res. 140 disapproving a Bureau of Land Management public land withdrawal on April 27, H.R. 8322 extending Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act authorities and H.R. 7816 curbing warrantless surveillance both on April 18, plus S. 1884 on Holocaust expropriated art recovery and S. 3971 reauthorizing small business innovation programs on April 13. With April ending and no further enrolled bills awaiting signature, traders assign modest probabilities to 4 (22.5%) or 5 (18.5%), viewing additional action unlikely absent emergency floor votes.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the CA-31 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-31 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 275M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

This follows a March DOE announcement of 172 million barrel releases coordinated with IEA partners, with solicitations continuing through 2027 to counter global shortages. High refinery utilization at 89.6% and OPEC+ modest May output hikes of 206,000 barrels per day add pressures, fueling trader consensus for further draws by June 5 resolution via EIA weekly reports, next due May 6 ahead of summer demand surge.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Bill Clinton divorce by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton announce their intention to divorce by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by the specified date of their intention to divorce will be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the divorce later actually occurs, or whether the actual divorce occurs outside of the timeframe of this market.

The resolution source will be statements from Bill Clinton and/or Hillary Clinton, and their representatives, however a definitive consensus of credible media reporting may be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 97.7% implied probability against a Bill Clinton divorce announcement by June 30, 2026, reflecting the absence of any public filings, legal proceedings, or official statements from the Clintons despite tabloid speculation tied to resurfaced Epstein documents in late 2025. Their 50-year marriage, marked by endurance through prior scandals like the Monica Lewinsky affair, continues amid shared Clinton Foundation activities, advanced age (both in their late 70s), and potential spousal privilege in legal contexts. No verifiable developments have emerged in the past 30 days to signal a split, with rumors fading without action. Realistic shifts would require an abrupt court filing or announcement in the next two months, such as escalated legal pressures or health events, though historical resilience suggests low risk.

With first rehearsals kicking off May 2, standout stagings could upend this tight pack before semis on May 12 and 14.

markets.question: Will Arouca qualify for the League Phase of the 2026-27 UEFA Conference League?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed team clinches a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League per UEFA rules. Otherwise, the associated market will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed team to clinch a league phase spot in the 2026-27 Conference League (e.g. they cannot mathematically achieve a Conference League place, cannot qualify through play in European or cup competitions, etc.), the associated market will resolve to "No". 

If the 2025-26 Primeira Liga season is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026 11:59 PM ET, or the teams that qualified for the 2026-27 Conference League have not been officially confirmed by UEFA within that timeframe, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA and/or the Primeira Liga; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: FC St. Pauli 1910 vs. 1. FSV Mainz 05
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for Sunday, May 3, 2026 between FC St. Pauli 1910 and 1. FSV Mainz 05.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight relegation battle positions define trader consensus for this Bundesliga survival clash at Millerntor-Stadion, with FC St. Pauli (16th, 31 points) hosting mid-table 1. FSV Mainz 05 (10th) in a matchup both desperately need. Recent 0-0 draw in the reverse fixture on December 21 underscores low-scoring, gritty dynamics, while St. Pauli's dismal form—minimal points from last six outings—clashes with home advantage against Mainz's inconsistent away results. Injuries plague both: hosts without Mathias Pereira Lage (recent complex knee), James Sands, Ricky-Jade Jones, and Simon Spari; visitors missing Maxim Dal, Jae-sung Lee (toe), and Maxim Leitsch (hamstring). Head-to-head favors Mainz (4 wins in last 5), yet balanced absences and stakes keep probabilities razor-thin at 36.5% Mainz, 35.5% St. Pauli, 28.5% draw.

eventMetadata.context_description: CFTC's March 2026 advisory has clarified listing standards for sports event contracts on Designated Contract Markets (DCMs), urging pre-self-certification engagement, sports league partnerships like the historic MLB MOU, and safeguards against manipulation using official data feeds. This guidance, amid surging prediction market volumes, positions established DCMs such as CME—bolstered by its MLB deal—and newcomers like recently registered Polymarket US to self-certify binary contracts on game outcomes by June 30. Kalshi led with listings in early 2025, but enhanced scrutiny and state challenges heighten uncertainty for others; traders eye upcoming filings and CFTC reviews as key catalysts for resolution.

markets.question: Will Meta have the best AI model at the end of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company which owns the model which has the highest arena rank based off the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on June 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" section on the Leaderboard tab of https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with the style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro at 66% to remain Venezuela's head of state by December 31, 2026, despite his January 3 capture by U.S. forces and ongoing detention in New York awaiting narcoterrorism trial, as Delcy Rodríguez's interim presidency—sworn in by the Supreme Court—nears expiration beyond constitutional 90-day limits without scheduled elections or formal transition. Recent U.S. sanctions relief on Rodríguez in early April and resumed commercial flights signal short-term stabilization, yet her April purges of Maduro allies and March military reshuffles highlight regime fractures, fueling skepticism over opposition figures like María Corina Machado (8.5%) or National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez gaining traction amid absent democratic commitments.

markets.question: Will Steve Hilton finish first in the 2026 California Governor primary election?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who receives the most valid votes in this primary election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including CBS/YouGov and Gudelunas Strategies surveys from late April, show Democrat Tom Steyer, former HHS Secretary Xavier Becerra, and Republican Steve Hilton clustered within single digits among likely primary voters, mirroring trader consensus on Polymarket where Steyer holds a slim 36.5% implied probability for first place ahead of the June 2 top-two primary. Steyer's massive self-funding—outpacing rivals—bolsters his position despite mixed polling, while Becerra surges on name recognition from his attorney general tenure and strong debate showings, and Hilton consolidates GOP support post-Eric Swalwell's withdrawal and amid Trump endorsement buzz. The race stays tight due to Democratic vote-splitting in a crowded field, with separation possible via endorsements, final debates, or shifts in undecideds (over 20% in polls).

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the third-most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a tight contest for third place in the proportional vote share of Russia's September 2026 State Duma election, with New People (NL), Liberal Democratic Party (LDPR), and Communist Party (KPRF) clustered at 31.5–34.5% implied probabilities behind dominant United Russia. Recent VCIOM and FOM polls from mid-April show these parties bunched at 8–17%, with NL gaining to 12–17% after approving its federal list led by Alexey Nechayev and Vladislav Davankov in March, while LDPR holds via patriotic anti-migrant messaging and KPRF faces reported regional interference. Divergent pollster methodologies and United Russia's dip to 27–52% sustain uncertainty; separation could arise from electronic primaries, campaign launches, or shifts in voter turnout amid economic pressures and war sentiment.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Governor Jim Pillen's near-certain trader consensus in the May 12 Republican primary reflects his entrenched position in solidly Republican Nebraska, bolstered by incumbency advantages and a fragmented field of lesser-known challengers like Sal Holguin, Sheila Korth-Focken, Gary L. Upsets remain possible via late scandals, health issues, or anomalous low-turnout favoring a niche challenger, though barriers are high with early voting underway.

markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0% and 0.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices UK Q1 2026 GDP growth in a tight contest, with 0.9-1.2% at 30.4%, 0.3-0.6% at 29.8%, and 1.2-1.5% at 28.4%, reflecting uncertainty over March monthly output amid mixed signals. February's stronger-than-expected 0.5% monthly GDP expansion—driven by services and production—lifted three-month growth to 0.5% through February, countering January's subdued 0.1% and supporting moderate bins, while April PMIs (manufacturing at 53.6, 47-month high) signal potential March upside. However, Bank of England April Monetary Policy Report projects underlying Q1 growth at just 0.2%, citing Middle East energy shocks elevating oil to $100/barrel and eroding confidence. Key swing factor: March GDP data, due May 14 alongside Q1 quarterly estimate.

If the 2026 MLB season is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

eventMetadata.context_description: Paul Skenes leads trader consensus at 31.5% implied probability to repeat as NL Cy Young winner, buoyed by his unanimous 2025 award, a sharp early 2026 start (4-1, 2.48 ERA, 0.72 WHIP, 30 strikeouts in 29 innings), and topping MLB's first Cy Young poll released April 29. Michael King's steady Padres outings (3-1, 2.41 ERA, 34 strikeouts) hold him at 16.3%, while Yoshinobu Yamamoto's 2-87 ERA in six starts supports 14.5%; all reflect hot April form amid thin innings totals and no major injury reports shaping the crowded race.

markets.question: Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by April 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, between Russia and Ukraine by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes," regardless of whether the ceasefire officially starts afterward.

Only ceasefires that constitute a general pause in the conflict will qualify. Ceasefires that only apply to energy infrastructure, the Black Sea, or other similar agreements will not qualify.

Any form of informal agreement will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A peace deal or political framework will qualify if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement, effective on a specific date. Frameworks or agreements that outline terms for a future peace but do not include an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not count.

This market's resolution will be based on official announcements from both Russia and Ukraine; however, a wide consensus of credible media reporting stating an official ceasefire agreement between Russia and Ukraine has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects near-certainty against a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire by April 30 amid ongoing military actions, including Ukrainian long-range drone strikes on Russian oil refineries as recently as April 29 and Russian drone-missile barrages targeting Ukrainian infrastructure. Stalled peace negotiations, highlighted by the abrupt end to February Geneva talks and mutual accusations over a short-lived Orthodox Easter truce earlier this month, underscore deep divisions over territorial control and security guarantees. President Putin's April 29 phone call with U.S. President Trump yielded a proposed May 9 Victory Day pause—post-deadline—but Ukraine is still seeking clarification without commitment. A dramatic last-hour diplomatic breakthrough remains the sole realistic shift, though entrenched frontline escalation and absent official announcements make it improbable.
